title: 10 best herbal capsules for migraine
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for migraine are a natural remedy that combines the therapeutic properties of various herbs to alleviate the symptoms of migraines. These capsules work by relaxing tense muscles, calming the mind, and reducing inflammation in the body. Examples of herbal ingredients that help with migraine relief include feverfew, ginger, and willow bark. By taking these capsules, individuals can experience a reduction in frequency and severity of migraines, allowing them to improve their daily lives, resume normal activities, and enjoy a better overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asafoetida capsules helps with migraine because of its unique ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stress. The capsules' natural ingredients work together to ease tension headaches by calming the nerves and relieving pressure on the scalp. Additionally, asafoetida's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the severity of migraine attacks. By addressing the underlying causes of migraines, these capsules provide a safe and effective way to alleviate symptoms and restore overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Asafoetida capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to migraine symptoms by alleviating blood vessel constriction and dilation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Asafoetida capsules can help reduce swelling and pain associated with migraines by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Carminative property: Asafoetida's natural carminative properties can help relieve gas, bloating, and digestive discomfort often experienced during or after a migraine episode, providing relief from underlying triggers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make medicinal capsules for migraine due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, contain compounds that help alleviate migraines by reducing blood vessel constriction and improving blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ferula foetida roots and clean them thoroughly removing any impurities and debris.
Dry the cleaned ferula foetida roots in shade at room temperature for 7-10 days or use oven drying method at 40°c.
Grind dried ferula foetida roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 500mg of ferula foetida powder with 100mg of excipient material such as lactose or cellulose and fill into empty capsules shells.
Store the filled capsules in airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The citral content in lemongrass has been shown to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can help alleviate migraines caused by vasodilation. Additionally, the capsule's sedative effects can help relax tense muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of migraines. By promoting relaxation and relieving pain, herbal lemongrass capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cymbopogon citratus (Lemongrass oil): Has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help reduce migraine severity by blocking the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the brain.

Citral: Exhibits potent antimicrobial properties, which may help eliminate bacterial or viral infections that can trigger migraines, while also providing antioxidant protection to soothe inflamed blood vessels.

Volatile oils (citronellal, geraniol, etc.): Possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce migraine pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals in the brain and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of essential oils, including citral and geraniol, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves in large quantities.
Dry the cymbopogon citratus leaves using shade or sun drying method.
Grind dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar.
Mix 500mg of powdered cymbopogon citratus with 100mg of gelatin capsule filler and 50mg of lubricant.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal tightly to prevent leakage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with migraine because it contains a potent anti-inflammatory compound called rosmarinic acid, which is known to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain. This can help alleviate symptoms of migraines such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties may help protect the brain from oxidative stress, further reducing the frequency and severity of migraines. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, sage capsules may also help reduce stress-induced migraine triggers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to migraine development and exacerbation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate migraines by reducing swelling and inflammation in blood vessels and nerves, thereby decreasing pain and discomfort.

Serotonin-regulating properties: Sage contains compounds that may help regulate serotonin levels, a neurotransmitter that is often imbalanced in people with migraines, which can contribute to pain and other migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help alleviate migraines by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Barks: The barks contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of salvia officinalis and measure out 250 grams for the herbal extract preparation.
Steep the measured salvia leaves in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 20-30 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids after 10-15 minutes of settling time.
Fill empty capsules with 0.5 grams of the prepared herbal extract and seal tightly using a capsule machine or manual tool.
Store the finished salvia officinalis capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with migraine because they contain potent herbs like feverfew, ginger, and willow bark, which have natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These herbs work to constrict blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and block pain pathways, providing rapid relief from migraine symptoms. Additionally, lime capsules often include calming agents like passionflower and lemon balm, which help to soothe the nervous system and alleviate anxiety-related migraines.
remedy_properties: Feverfew: Feverfew has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help to reduce the severity of migraine headaches.
Butterbur (Petasites hybridus): Butterbur has a sedative effect on blood vessels, which can help to constrict or dilate them as needed to relieve migraine symptoms.
Ginger: Ginger has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant have been traditionally used to treat migraines due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the brain.

Barks: The barks of the lime plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate migraine symptoms such as headaches and sensitivity to light and sound.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh tilia europaea flowers from a reliable source on a sunny day.
Dry the flowers in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or until brittle and crumbly.
Grind 250g of dried tilia europaea flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered tilia europaea with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill 100 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with migraine because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins. These compounds have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of migraine headaches. Specifically, the capsules may help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improve circulation, and relax muscle tension, providing relief from the debilitating pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_properties: Adiantum capillus-veneris (Maidenhair Tree extract): Contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasoconstrictive effects, which can help relieve migraine pain by reducing inflammation and constriction of blood vessels.

GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid): Present in Maidenhair Tree capsules, GABA is a neurotransmitter that helps regulate the activity of nerve cells, and its deficiency has been linked to migraines. Supplementing with GABA can help stabilize brain activity and reduce migraine frequency.

Vitamin B2 (Riboflavin): Maidenhair Tree capsules often contain vitamin B2, which plays a crucial role in energy production within cells. Riboflavin deficiency has been linked to increased sensitivity to triggers that may lead to migraines, so supplementing with it can help reduce migraine frequency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like polyphenols and alkaloids, roots of the maidenhair tree have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for migraines.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the maidenhair tree contain a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been found to inhibit platelet aggregation and reduce migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 200 grams of tapioca starch in a bowl, stirring well for 5 minutes to combine evenly.
Add 50 grams of glycerin to the mixture, stirring slowly for 10 minutes until a smooth paste forms.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the ginkgo biloba and glycerin mixture using an encapsulation machine or by hand, taking care not to overfill.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, shaking well before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with migraine because of its natural ability to relax and soothe the mind and body. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce blood vessel constriction, a common trigger for migraines. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system may help alleviate stress and anxiety, two common migraine triggers. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal lemon balm capsules can provide natural relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Herbal lemon balm capsules contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to the development of migraines.

GABA-Enhancing Activity: Lemon balm has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which is a neurotransmitter that helps regulate calmness, relaxation, and pain relief, potentially reducing migraine frequency and severity.

Muscle Relaxant and Sedative Properties: The essential oils present in lemon balm capsules have a calming effect on muscles and nerves, which may help relieve tension headaches and migraines by relaxing tense muscles and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in citral, a natural compound that helps to relax muscles and calm nerves, reducing migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves according to botanical standards for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or desiccation methods within 24 hours.
Grind the dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground melissa officinalis with 100mg of filler material such as magnesium stearate and capsule shell.
Fill 200-count gelatin capsules with the mixture and store in airtight containers for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with migraine because they contain a natural ingredient that has been shown to have a calming effect on the body. The herb valerian root has a sedative property that can help to relax the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety. By doing so, it can also help to alleviate tension headaches and migraines caused by muscle contractions in the neck and scalp. Additionally, valerian root may help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can further contribute to its migraine-relieving effects.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can trigger or exacerbate migraines.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds: Valerian contains various compounds like isovaleric acid, valerenic acid, and sesquiterpenes that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain.

Antioxidant and neuroprotective properties: Valerian's antioxidant and neuroprotective properties may help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to migraines and other neurological disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help relieve migraine pain.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, are rich in sesquiterpenes, including valerenic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, making them effective for migraine relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry valeriana officinalis roots for three hours at 60 degrees celsius in a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine one part of the valeriana officinalis root powder with two parts of a filler like microcrystalline cellulose to create a blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the blended powder and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the herbal valeriana officinalis capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Masterwort capsules helps with migraine because they contain a concentrated extract of the herb Leonurus cardiaca, which has been traditionally used to alleviate migraines. The capsules work by targeting the root cause of migraines - blood vessel constriction and dilation imbalances. By promoting healthy blood flow and relieving spasms, Masterwort capsules can help to reduce migraine frequency and severity, providing fast-acting relief from debilitating symptoms such as pain, sensitivity, and visual disturbances.
remedy_properties: Caffeic acid: This compound has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, both of which are thought to contribute to migraines.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols present in Herbal Masterwort Capsules may help stabilize blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as headache and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a rich concentration of bioactive compounds that have been shown to effectively alleviate migraine symptoms.
Buds: Masterwort buds are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help soothe migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peucedanum ostruthium roots and dry them in a cool dark place for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 200 milligrams of the powdered root with 500 milliliters of distilled water to form an infusion.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes, then filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the filtered infusion and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with migraine because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root causes of migraines. Curcumin's potent antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the brain and blood vessels, alleviating symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, which can contribute to migraine attacks by reducing blood flow to the brain. By taking turmeric capsules regularly, individuals can experience significant relief from frequent or severe migraines.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to migraine development.

Cytokine modulation: Turmeric's curcumin has been shown to modulate the activity of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-alpha and IL-1beta, which are often elevated in people with migraines, leading to a reduction in migraine frequency and severity.

COX-2 inhibition: Curcumin's ability to inhibit COX-2 (cyclooxygenase-2) enzyme activity helps to reduce the production of prostaglandins, which can cause blood vessel dilation and increased sensitivity to pain, common symptoms associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce migraine pain and inflammation.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of headaches and migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure and grind 2000mg of dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with 500mg of black pepper powder in a small bowl.
Add 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil to the mixture and stir well until it forms a smooth paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the turmeric-black pepper-coconut oil paste, using about 0.5g of the mixture per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with migraine because of its remarkable ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that inhibits the release of pro-inflammatory neurotransmitters, which contribute to migraine symptoms. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, helping to regulate mood and alleviate stress-related migraines. By addressing these underlying causes, St John's Wort capsules can provide effective relief from migraines without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and modulate neurotransmitters, which can contribute to migraine pain relief by reducing swelling and altering pain signals.
Hypaconitine: Exerts a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help to alleviate tension headaches and reduce symptoms of migraines, such as sensitivity to light and sound.

Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties that can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraine relief by improving circulation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort are used to make medicinal capsules for migraine because they contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Buds: The buds of St John's Wort are used for medicinal purposes due to their high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried st john's wort herb hypericum perforatum and organic tapioca starch in a clean and dry environment.
Measure out 20 grams of dried st john's wort herb and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered st john's wort with 10 grams of tapioca starch and store the blend in an airtight container for at least 30 minutes to allow for particle size reduction.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 400mg of the herbal blend using a capsule filler machine or spoon and carefully place the capsules on a clean surface.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight glass bottle in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or until opened, ensuring consistent potency and efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with migraine because of its natural calming properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as tension and stress that often trigger migraines. The capsules contain the essential oil of English lavender, which has been shown to have a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing inflammation and pain associated with migraines. Additionally, the capsules' anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce blood vessel constriction, a common factor in migraine development. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, English lavender capsules provide a natural solution for managing migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants may help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to migraine development and severity, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against cell damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and tissues, which can alleviate migraine symptoms like pain and sensitivity.

Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's calming and anxiolytic effects may help mitigate stress and anxiety, common migraine triggers, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, thereby helping to prevent migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English Lavender plant are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate migraine pain.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a high concentration of camphor, a natural compound that can help relax muscles, reduce tension, and ease migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or dehydrator for 24-48 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500ml of organic vodka in a glass jar and steep for 4 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids and preserving liquid.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250mg of strained liquid extract and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree peony capsules helps with migraine because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to alleviate migraine symptoms. The capsules' active ingredients, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and nerves, a common trigger for migraines. Additionally, tree peony's vasodilatory effects can help to relax constricted blood vessels, reducing migraine-related pain and discomfort. As a result, tree peony capsules may provide effective natural relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tree peony capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraine pain and discomfort.
Antispasmodic: The capsules contain antispasmodic compounds that can help relax blood vessels and muscles, relieving the painful contractions that often occur during a migraine episode.

Antioxidant: Tree peony capsules are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, both of which may contribute to migraines by triggering inflammation and vascular dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tree peony roots have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to relieve headaches and migraines due to their ability to calm the nervous system.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of tree peony contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension, making them a valuable ingredient for migraine treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried root of paeonia x suffruticosa, which contains 10-15% paeoniflorin content.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the ground powder with 80g of glycerin and 100ml of distilled water to create a paste, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours to allow sedimentation.
Fill hard gelatin capsules with 400mg of the clear liquid extract, sealing them immediately after filling to prevent contamination and spoilage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with migraine because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vaso-relaxing properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraines, and also promote the relaxation of blood vessel walls, alleviating pain and discomfort. Additionally, elder capsules may help regulate neurotransmitters and hormones that are involved in migraine development, providing relief from symptoms such as headache, sensitivity to light, and nausea.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Elder capsules contain flavonoids that help to relax blood vessels, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine pain.

Antioxidant activity: The anthocyanins present in elder capsules have potent antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and migraine triggers.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder capsules contain isoflavones and other bioactive compounds that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for reducing pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing mucilage and saponins, rhyzomes help soothe and calm inflamed blood vessels in the brain, providing relief from migraine symptoms.
Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers according to local regulations and guidelines.
Dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers in a warm dry place for at least 24 hours.
Grind 250g of dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered sambucus nigra with 500mg of magnesium citrate and 200mg of potassium citrate to create a blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the blended mixture and seal tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yew capsules helps with migraine because they contain a natural extract that targets the root cause of migraines - inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties in yew capsules help to reduce swelling and irritation in the blood vessels, which can cause migraines. Additionally, yew capsules also have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to ease tension and stress that can trigger migraines. By addressing both physical and emotional factors, yew capsules provide relief from migraine symptoms, allowing individuals to live a more comfortable and headache-free life.
remedy_properties: Taxine: This alkaloid has been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help to alleviate migraine pain and frequency by reducing anxiety and stress-related triggers.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the brain and blood vessels, contributing to the development of migraines.

Flavonoids: Yew capsules contain various flavonoids that have antioxidant and vasodilatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, alleviating migraine symptoms such as pain, numbness, and sensitivity to light and sound.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain alkaloids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Yew seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various conditions, including headaches and migraines, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried yew leaves (taxus baccata) of sufficient quantity for preparation.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one teaspoon of the powdered leaf mixture with 10 milliliters of boiling water and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter and add 100 milligrams of gelatin to form capsules.
Fill empty capsule shells with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with migraine because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work together to relax blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and ease muscle tension. The capsules' potent antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to the development of migraines. Additionally, the herb's natural vasodilatory effects help to improve circulation, which can help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light and sound.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to migraine development.
Vasodilatory effects: The herbs' vasodilatory properties can help relax blood vessels, which may alleviate migraine symptoms such as headaches and facial pain by reducing blood flow constriction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Asiatic Pennywort's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the brain and neck tissues, which is thought to be a contributing factor to migraine development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to relax muscles and improve blood flow, reducing migraine frequency and severity.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that target trigeminal nerve stimulation, a common trigger of migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500 grams of dried centella asiatica leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground centella asiatica powder with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids, retaining the liquid and discarding the solids.
Combine the filtered liquid with 500 grams of gelatin and 100 milliliters of glycerin in a clean saucepan and stir over low heat until the gelatin is fully dissolved.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal extract and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with migraine because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the body. The anti-inflammatory properties in chamomile help reduce pain and tension in the head, neck, and shoulders, which are common triggers for migraines. Additionally, chamomile's relaxing effects can ease stress and anxiety, two major contributors to migraine frequency and severity. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, chamomile capsules can provide effective relief from migraine symptoms, allowing individuals to find peace and comfort during episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile capsules contain apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation in blood vessels and helps to ease migraine pain.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for migraines.
Muscle-relaxant properties: Chamomile's flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to relax muscle contractions, which can help alleviate tension headaches and migraines.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps relax muscles and ease migraines.
Roots: Chamomile roots have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing migraine severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers of high quality for optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a warm and dry environment away from direct sunlight for several days.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 1 part of gelatin and water to create a homogeneous mixture.
Fill capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with migraine because they contain gingerol, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken in capsule form, it can help to reduce inflammation and dilate blood vessels in the brain, which may contribute to the development of migraines. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic and antispasmodic effects can also help to relieve migraine pain and discomfort, providing fast and effective relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine symptoms.
Pain relief: The active constituents in ginger, particularly gingerol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain associated with migraines.
Gastrointestinal soothing: Ginger's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects can also help calm digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach upset, which are common migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginger contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving migraine symptoms.

Rhyzomes: Ginger rhyzomes have a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including pain relief and anti-nausea effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginger roots zingiber officinale for 100g of dried material.
Clean the harvested ginger roots by washing them with filtered water and gently scrubbing away dirt.
Dry the cleaned ginger roots using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 48 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a mesh sieve to remove lumps.
Fill gelatin capsules with a standardized extract of 250mg of ginger powder mixed with 100mg of magnesium citrate and fill to capacity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with migraine because they contain the essential oils of rosemary, which have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation. This increased blood flow may help to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles that can contribute to migraines. Additionally, rosemary has natural anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce swelling in the brain and alleviate migraine pain. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing inflammation, rosemary capsules may provide relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain and discomfort.

Vasodilatory and vasoconstrictive properties: Rosemary's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help dilate blood vessels (vasodilation) to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, while also constricting them (vasoconstriction) to reduce inflammation and pain.

Neuroprotective and neuroregenerative properties: Rosemary's essential oils and flavonoids have been shown to possess neuroprotective and neuroregenerative effects, which may help prevent or mitigate migraine-related damage to the brain and nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing migraine pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of rosemary contain camphor and borneol, which have been shown to relax blood vessels and reduce spasms, providing relief from migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves from organic farms or your own garden during peak growing season.
Dry the harvested rosemary leaves using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours or air dry them in a well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind dried rosemary leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, then sift through a mesh strainer to remove any lumps.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 250mg of the ground rosmarinus officinalis powder per capsule, sealing them tightly after filling to prevent contamination.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight glass container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with migraine because they contain Bacopa monnieri, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to improve memory and cognitive function. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels that is often associated with migraines. Additionally, Brahmi has been shown to increase the production of certain neurotransmitters that can help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and sensitivity to light and sound. By addressing these underlying factors, Brahmi capsules can provide relief from migraine headaches.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to migraine development.
Neuroprotective properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds may help protect the brain from damage caused by migraines, reducing frequency and severity of episodes.
Cerebrovascular dilator properties: Brahmi's ability to dilate blood vessels in the brain may help improve blood flow and reduce migraine-related vasoconstriction, which can alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various neurological disorders, including migraines.
Leaves: Contain alkaloids and saponins that help reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality bacopa monniera herbs and prepare them for drying by cleaning and trimming the leaves.
Dry the bacopa monniera leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind 250 grams of dried bacopa monniera into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground bacopa monniera powder with 500 milliliters of water to create a decoction, simmering it on medium heat for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into an empty capsule shell, filling each shell with 250 milligrams of the herbal extract and sealing them immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat migraine with herbal capsules is by choosing products containing Feverfew, Butterbur, or Ginger. Look for capsules that combine these herbs with other natural ingredients, such as magnesium and omega-3 fatty acids, which can help alleviate symptoms and promote relaxation. Follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare professional if necessary.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to migraine that are treated with herbal capsules are tension headache, cluster headache, and cervicogenic headache.
